data.narrative:
Quobly is a seed-stage quantum computing company founded by Maud Vinet, Tristan Meunier, and François Perruchot. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 known comparable companies: 3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private entities including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to Quobly. The quantum computing peer set contains only 7 same-sector companies, but zero meet the filtering criteria of matching stage (seed), adjacent stage (early-stage), or similar valuation tier simultaneously. This absence of sufficient narrow comparables—requiring a minimum of 3 datapoints for statistical validity—precludes reliable peer-median extrapolation. An all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of quantum technology approaches and commercialization timelines across the peer universe.

No triangulated valuation range can be derived from the available data. The analysis is constrained by the absence of: (1) disclosed seed-stage quantum computing transactions with public pricing; (2) sufficient same-stage peers within the sector; and (3) quantitative metrics linking Quobly's technical progress or team composition to comparable funding events. Resolution of valuation for this target would require either expansion of disclosed transaction data within quantum computing seed funding, or application of alternative methods (e.g., venture capital method with explicit assumptions on exit multiple and probability) tied to sector-specific benchmarks.
